title: Open WebUI Integration Adapter Pattern

decision.summary: Use Open WebUI as a shell and access surface only. Place the Ageix Human Interface Adapter inside Ageix or as an Ageix-owned sidecar, exposed through narrow OpenAPI first and optionally governed MCP second.

placement_preference_order.reason: Keeps governance, authorization, audit, and state ownership in Ageix.
Can preserve ownership if code is versioned, reviewed, and governed as Ageix adapter code.
Arbitrary Python execution, upgrade fragility, and authority drift risk.

adapter_contract.optional_external_surface: MCP Streamable HTTP for constrained governed capability access

project_context.enforcement: Adapter rejects or normalizes Ageix-specific requests unless project_id is exactly Ageix. Open WebUI workspace, chat, model, or group context is never treated as project authority.

identity_context.source: OIDC/SSO claims, OAuth tool authorization, gateway identity, or Open WebUI header templates where available

not_allowed_sources: Open WebUI button click without rationale where policy requires rationale

allowed_initial_26_2_surface.name: Decision Inbox MVP
allowed_data: next governed action labels without executable mutation

invariants: Ageix remains the system of record.
No direct UI-to-worker mutation path is introduced.
No direct UI-to-repository mutation path is introduced.
No separate approval store is introduced.
Open WebUI RBAC controls shell access only and does not authorize Ageix decisions.
All Ageix state-changing requests route through governed Ageix capabilities.
All Ageix-specific requests explicitly carry project_id Ageix.
Future mutations require explicit human rationale where governance requires rationale.
No dependency on the unfinished intent engine is introduced.

risk_summary.mitigation: Avoid production mutation paths in Tools, Functions, Pipes, Filters, or Pipelines. Use Ageix-owned adapter.
Ageix validates identity and authorizes capability calls at the adapter boundary.
Decision Inbox is structured and read-only; chat/tool calls may supplement but not define workflow state.
No approval state or governance authority is stored in Open WebUI.
Avoid Open WebUI core forks/custom pages for 26.2; prefer stable external adapter contracts.

validation_expectations: Confirm all 26.2 Decision Inbox endpoints are read-only.
Confirm project_id Ageix is required and enforced.
Confirm unauthorized users cannot see privileged records or controls.
Confirm Open WebUI stores no approval or decision authority state.
Confirm no shell, Git, worker, or repository mutation code is introduced.
Confirm no unreviewed Open WebUI community plugins are required.
